raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A general section review centrally includes checking whether the routing motivation, mechanism, and evaluation attribution form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The section makes specific empirical claims about p99 latency and classifier error, so reviewing it includes checking whether those claims are aligned with the reported evidence.","R1.5":"Because the artifact is an entire paper section, review responsibility includes assessing section-level ordering and narrative progression.","R1.6":"Reviewing a section load-bearingly entails sentence- and paragraph-level polish, including grammar, flow, tense consistency, and concision.","RX.1":"Any review projection must disclose uncertainty where the broader paper context, experiments, or prior work are unavailable.","RX.2":"The projection must preserve responsibility for identifying or avoiding unsupported claims such as causal attribution and first-system assertions.","RX.3":"The agent must remain within reviewing the provided section rather than executing edits or expanding into unrelated paper tasks.","RX.4":"The review responsibility includes avoiding guidance that could encourage misleading empirical, operational, or scholarly claims.","RX.5":"The review should remain traceable to statements present in the provided artifact and distinguish them from assumptions about missing sections."}}

rationale.R1.2: A general section review centrally includes checking whether the routing motivation, mechanism, and evaluation attribution form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes specific empirical claims about p99 latency and classifier error, so reviewing it includes checking whether those claims are aligned with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.5: Because the artifact is an entire paper section, review responsibility includes assessing section-level ordering and narrative progression.
rationale.R1.6: Reviewing a section load-bearingly entails sentence- and paragraph-level polish, including grammar, flow, tense consistency, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any review projection must disclose uncertainty where the broader paper context, experiments, or prior work are unavailable.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must preserve responsibility for identifying or avoiding unsupported claims such as causal attribution and first-system assertions.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must remain within reviewing the provided section rather than executing edits or expanding into unrelated paper tasks.
rationale.RX.4: The review responsibility includes avoiding guidance that could encourage misleading empirical, operational, or scholarly claims.
rationale.RX.5: The review should remain traceable to statements present in the provided artifact and distinguish them from assumptions about missing sections.
